engineGuide.h2: Jaguar E-Pace Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Jaguar E-Pace across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Introduced in 2017 as JLR's core Ingenium diesel, delivering strong low-end torque but later noted for timing chain wear.
compatibleVariants: D150 S, D150 SE, D150 R-Dynamic, D180 S, D180 SE, D180 R-Dynamic, D180 HSE
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and plastic guide failure (typically 60k-80k miles)
EGR cooler cracking leading to coolant loss
Injector seal carbon buildup
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204DTD Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Updated Ingenium block with revised emissions hardware and improved oil pump, powering the majority of D200 models.
compatibleVariants: D165 SE, D165 R-Dynamic, D200 S, D200 SE, D200 R-Dynamic, D200 HSE, D200 First Edition
commonFailures: Turbocharger actuator sticking or motor failure
DPF sensor fault causing regeneration interruption
Water pump impeller degradation
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ200D4 Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mid-cycle Ingenium diesel variant refined for Euro 6d compliance, offering smoother operation and reduced NVH levels.
compatibleVariants: D180 SE, D180 R-Dynamic, D180 HSE, D200 R-Dynamic, D200 HSE, D200 Black Edition
commonFailures: Mild-hybrid belt starter generator bearing noise
AdBlue injector crystallisation
Oil pressure sensor false readings
entries.cta: Get quotes for ING-20D-200 Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: High-output pre-facelift diesel delivering peak torque for towing and performance, discontinued after the 2020 model year.
compatibleVariants: D240 R-Dynamic, D240 HSE, D240 First Edition
commonFailures: Twin sequential turbocharger failure (primary or secondary)
High-pressure fuel pump wear
Piston ring glazing from short-trip urban driving
entries.cta: Get quotes for ING-20D-240 Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Versatile four-cylinder turbo petrol forming the backbone of P200 and P250 ranges, balancing power with daily drivability.
compatibleVariants: P200 S, P200 SE, P200 R-Dynamic, P200 HSE, P250 R-Dynamic, P250 HSE, P250 First Edition
commonFailures: Direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves
Turbo wastegate rattle and linkage wear
Thermostat housing crack causing coolant leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ200P4 Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level turbo petrol code standardising the 200hp tune, widely adopted post-2020 facelift for improved fuel economy.
compatibleVariants: P200 SE, P200 R-Dynamic, P200 HSE, P200 Black
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump premature wear
Timing chain tensioner loss of preload
Oil dilution in colder climates from rich cold-start maps

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Reinforced turbo petrol variant with twin-scroll charging, delivering sustained high-output performance across trim levels.
compatibleVariants: P250 R-Dynamic, P250 HSE, P250 First Edition, P250 Black
commonFailures: Intercooler piping split under high boost pressure
Valve cover gasket oil seepage
Variable valve timing solenoid sticking

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Pre-facelift flagship petrol delivering 300hp, known for rapid acceleration and higher thermal stress under sustained load.
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing failure due to oil starvation
Crankcase ventilation valve diaphragm rupture
Spark plug electrode degradation causing misfires

entries.history: Compact three-cylinder mild-hybrid unit introduced to lower fleet emissions while maintaining responsive turbo performance.
compatibleVariants: P160 SE, P160 R-Dynamic, P160 HSE

HPFP high-frequency vibration damage

Inverter cooling pump motor burnout

commonProblems.h2: Jaguar E Pace Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Jaguar E Pace Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Jaguar E Pace Timing Chain Failure - Why Ingenium Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar E Pace D150, D165, D180, D200 (2017-2024, 204DTD & AJ200D4); Jaguar E Pace P200, P250, P300 (2017-2024, AJ200P4 & ING-20P series)
problems.rootCause: The rear‑mounted timing chain tensioners rely on consistent oil pressure and specific factory oil viscosity. Over time, plastic guide rails become brittle and tensioners lose preload, causing chain slack, cam timing drift, and eventual interference with valves.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain & Tensioner Upgrade
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine-out access to rear casing, replacement of chain, updated metal/plastic hybrid guides, and upgraded hydraulic tensioners
repairOptions.longevity: Highly effective if caught early before valve contact. Adds 60k-80k miles of reliable operation

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, bent valve replacement, machining, timing kit install, and full diagnostic recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if damage is isolated to inlet/exhaust valves. Risk of hidden block scoring remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a fully reconditioned Ingenium unit with reinforced timing components, new ancillaries, bench testing, and ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution. Eliminates legacy tensioner weakness and restores factory performance with full warranty cover

problems.recommendation: For a Jaguar E Pace approaching 60,000 miles with early chain rattle on cold starts, we strongly advise investing in the preventative timing chain upgrade. If the chain has already jumped or metallic debris is circulating through the oil galleries, targeted repairs rarely succeed long‑term. A rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioners and guides is the most cost‑effective way to secure the next 80,000+ miles without recurring dealer‑level bills.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Ingenium engine prices for your Jaguar E Pace

problems.group: Jaguar E Pace EGR Cooler Failure & DPF Clogging
problems.h4: Jaguar E Pace EGR Cooler Failure & DPF Clogging - Diesel Emissions System Risks
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar E Pace D150, D165, D180, D200, D240 (2017-2024, 204DTD, AJ200D4, ING-20D series)
problems.rootCause: Short urban trips prevent the diesel particulate filter from completing active regeneration cycles, while carbon buildup clogs the EGR cooler matrix. This causes coolant contamination, restricted exhaust flow, and excessive backpressure that strains turbo bearings and piston rings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical and ultrasonic DPF regeneration, EGR cooler replacement, sensor calibration, and forced service regeneration cycle
repairOptions.longevity: Restores emissions compliance and MPG if caught before soot saturation hardens or coolant enters cylinders

repairOptions.tier: Advanced - Turbo & Intake Cleaning + DPF Swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo inspection for oil starvation damage, intake manifold decarbonising, OEM DPF replacement, and EGR routing updates (where compliant)
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses secondary damage but does not resolve core block wear from chronic oil contamination

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Diesel Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete Ingenium diesel replacement with updated EGR cooler routing, high‑flow DPF pre‑fitting, full ancillary service, and 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive fix for engines suffering chronic regeneration failure or cylinder wall scoring from diluted oil

problems.recommendation: If your Jaguar E Pace diesel shows only warning lights and reduced power, a specialist DPF and EGR service will likely resolve it cost‑effectively. However, if you notice coolant loss mixed with oil or persistent black smoke after cleaning, the block and piston rings are likely compromised. At that stage, replacing the engine with a pre‑serviced, emissions‑compliant rebuilt unit prevents costly repeat failures and ensures MOT compliance.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar E Pace D180 D200 replacement engines

problems.h4: Jaguar E Pace Turbocharger Failure - Performance Loss Under Load
problems.rootCause: High‑output petrol and pre‑facelift twin‑turbo diesel variants operate at elevated boost pressures and exhaust temperatures. Oil coking in the turbo feed lines starves the centre bearing, while the electronic wastegate actuator develops internal short circuits or linkage play, causing sudden power loss and limp‑mode engagement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of existing turbo unit, installation of upgraded ball‑bearing or hybrid turbo core, oil line flush, and actuator recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost pressure and towing capability if the core block and oil pump remain healthy

repairOptions.tier: Advanced - Full Breeder & Oil System Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: High‑pressure oil pump inspection, PCV valve replacement, intercooler cleaning, and turbo oil feed line upgrade to prevent future coking
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary preventative step if oil dilution or sludge is present, but does not fix existing bearing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine supply & fit with reinforced turbo feed/return lines, updated wastegate assembly, full fluid flush, and performance mapping
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when turbo failure has sent debris into the intake or caused oil starvation damage to the crankshaft bearings

problems.recommendation: On a Jaguar E Pace used for frequent motorway driving or towing, turbo failure often stems from neglected oil service intervals. If the failure is isolated to the turbocharger itself, a quality replacement unit combined with an oil system flush is sufficient. However, if metallic grinding preceded the failure or oil pressure warnings flashed beforehand, the engine internals are likely scored. In those cases, a rebuilt engine with upgraded oil management components is the only reliable long‑term solution.
problems.cta: Compare Jaguar E Pace P250 P300 rebuilt engine prices

problems.group: Jaguar E Pace High‑Pressure Fuel Pump Failure & Petrol Oil Dilution
problems.h4: Jaguar E Pace High‑Pressure Fuel Pump Failure & Petrol Oil Dilution - Direct Injection Risks
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar E Pace P200, P160 (2020-2024, ING-20P-200, AJ150P3); Jaguar E Pace P300e Hybrid (2020-2024, ING-15PHEV)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection combined with frequent short trips and EV‑mode operation on PHEV variants causes unburned fuel to wash down cylinder walls and mix with engine oil. This dilutes oil viscosity, accelerates cam lobe and bearing wear, and causes premature failure of the high‑pressure fuel pump due to lubrication loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: High‑pressure fuel pump replacement, complete oil and filter change with manufacturer‑spec low‑SAPS oil, and ECU software update for cold‑start enrichment
repairOptions.longevity: Effective preventative measure for early‑dilution symptoms. Must be paired with revised service intervals (5,000-7,000 miles)

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Sump removal, main and big‑end bearing measurement, walnut blasting intake valves to remove carbon, and pump line pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if bearing clearances remain within factory tolerance. Does not guarantee against future cam wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete petrol replacement with upgraded HPFP seals, revised piston ring tensioners, full decarbonised assembly, and hybrid battery interface testing (where applicable)
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates dilution‑induced block wear and restores compression. Essential once knocking or low oil pressure is detected

problems.recommendation: If you own a post‑2020 Jaguar E Pace petrol or PHEV and notice the oil level rising on the dipstick, treat it as an urgent warning. Switching to shorter service intervals and using OEM‑approved fuel additives can sometimes prevent catastrophic damage. Once bearing knock or cam lobe scoring occurs, partial repairs become financially unviable due to the precision tolerances of modern direct‑injection blocks. A rebuilt engine with reinforced fuel system components restores reliability and protects your vehicle’s residual value.
problems.cta: Compare Jaguar E Pace P160 P200 engine replacement quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jaguar E Pace
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your Jaguar E Pace is struggling with timing chain rattle, turbo lag, DPF blockage, or direct‑injection oil dilution, waiting rarely reduces repair costs and often turns manageable issues into complete block failures. EngineMarket connects you directly with vetted UK specialists who stock and fit precision‑rebuilt Ingenium engines, eliminating main dealer markups while guaranteeing MOT‑compliant performance. Every unit undergoes rigorous bench testing, features upgraded weak‑point components, and arrives with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes from our nationwide network today and secure a reliable, cost‑effective solution to get your E Pace back on the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jaguar E Pace Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jaguar E Pace and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Jaguar E Pace Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every official displacement fitted to the Jaguar E‑Pace across all UK generations is detailed below, complete with matching engine codes, variant compatibility windows and documented failure notes.

groups.title: Jaguar E Pace Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Jaguar E Pace 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0‑litre Ingenium diesel forms the absolute backbone of the E‑Pace range, powering every diesel variant from the efficiency‑focused entry models to the high‑torque pre‑facelift D240. Fitted across the entire X540 chassis lifespan, this compact twin‑turbo architecture pairs an aluminium block with advanced emissions hardware to deliver strong low‑end pulling power and genuine motorway economy.
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar E Pace 2.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Jaguar E Pace Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Jaguar E Pace 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Introduced during the 2020 mid‑cycle refresh to meet tighter WLTP emissions standards, the 1.5‑litre three‑cylinder Ingenium petrol provides a lightweight, highly responsive powertrain for urban and commuter driving. Exclusively deployed in P160 mild‑hybrid applications, it utilises a 48V belt‑driven starter generator to smooth out turbo spool characteristics and reduce cold‑start fuel consumption.
commonFailurePoints: mild oil dilution from repeated short journeys
items.cta: Compare Jaguar E Pace 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar E Pace 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0‑litre four‑cylinder petrol block serves as the mainstream and performance heart of the petrol lineup, spanning the daily‑driver P200 through to the pre‑facelift 300PS flagship. Its direct‑injection architecture, lightweight aluminium construction and seamless ZF 9‑speed transmission pairing deliver brisk acceleration and refined high‑speed cruising without compromising everyday practicality.
commonFailurePoints: Intake valve carbon buildup from direct injection
items.cta: Compare Jaguar E Pace 2.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Jaguar E Pace Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Jaguar E Pace 1.5L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Engineered specifically for UK drivers navigating urban clean‑air zones without sacrificing long‑distance touring capability, this plug‑in hybrid configuration pairs a highly efficient three‑cylinder petrol block with an integrated electric traction motor and liquid‑cooled high‑voltage battery pack. Available exclusively as the P300e, the system delivers silent EV‑only city commuting while retaining the familiar Ingenium character once the combustion unit engages at higher speeds.
commonFailurePoints: crankcase oil contamination from extended EV‑mode usage
items.cta: Enquire about Jaguar E Pace 1.5L hybrid engine availability

faq.h2: Jaguar E-Pace Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement costs typically range from £1,600 to £4,500 for rebuilt units, depending on variant, engine code and whether supply-only or supply & fit is selected. Diesel D180 and petrol P250 replacements dominate UK enquiries.
keyPoints: Rebuilt Jaguar E-Pace engines cost £1,600-£4,500 supply only
Supply & fit adds £600-£1,200 labour depending on location
D180 diesel and P250 petrol are most commonly replaced variants
Prices vary by engine code, mileage and specialist availability
items.cta: Compare Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Which Jaguar E-Pace engine is most reliable?
items.answer: Post-2021 facelift Jaguar E-Pace engines with updated Ingenium components show improved reliability, particularly MHEV petrol variants. Early pre-facelift D180 and P300 units require proactive timing chain maintenance to avoid premature failure.
keyPoints: 2021+ Jaguar E-Pace MHEV engines feature revised tensioners
Pre-facelift D180 requires timing chain inspection by 60k miles
P160 three-cylinder shows strong early reliability data
Regular oil changes critical for all Jaguar E-Pace Ingenium engines
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable rebuilt Jaguar E-Pace engines

items.question: What causes Jaguar E-Pace timing chain failure?
items.answer: Jaguar E-Pace timing chain failure stems from rear-mounted tensioner wear and plastic guide degradation, typically between 45k-85k miles. Early rattling on cold starts is the primary warning sign before catastrophic interference occurs.
keyPoints: Jaguar E-Pace timing chain tensioners lose preload over time
Plastic guide rails become brittle and shed debris into oil
Failure often occurs between 45,000-85,000 miles on D180/P250
Metal debris circulation can score bearings beyond repair
items.cta: Check rebuilt Jaguar E-Pace engines with upgraded timing components

items.question: Is a reconditioned Jaguar E-Pace engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes, a properly reconditioned Jaguar E-Pace engine from a vetted UK specialist is highly reliable, featuring upgraded internal components that address original failure points like timing guides and EGR hardware, backed by 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt Jaguar E-Pace engines include upgraded tensioners and guides
Bench testing verifies oil pressure and compression before dispatch
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across EngineMarket network
Specialist rebuilds often exceed original factory specification durability
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Jaguar E-Pace engine prices with warranty

items.question: Should I choose petrol or diesel for my Jaguar E-Pace replacement?
items.answer: Choose diesel Jaguar E-Pace engines for high-mileage motorway use and towing; select petrol for urban driving and lower emissions compliance complexity. Both Ingenium families share similar failure patterns requiring specialist rebuilding expertise.
keyPoints: Jaguar E-Pace diesel excels at motorway economy and torque
Petrol variants avoid DPF/AdBlue complexity on short journeys
Both fuel types require timing chain preventative maintenance
Emissions compliance identical for rebuilt Jaguar E-Pace engines
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar E-Pace diesel or petrol replacement engines

items.question: How long will a replacement Jaguar E-Pace engine last?
items.answer: A rebuilt Jaguar E-Pace engine with upgraded components typically delivers 80,000-120,000 miles of reliable service when maintained to manufacturer intervals, with many owners exceeding original engine lifespan through proactive oil and filter changes.
keyPoints: Rebuilt Jaguar E-Pace engines often outlast original units
5,000-7,000 mile oil changes maximise longevity
Upgraded tensioners and guides reduce repeat failure risk
Warranty coverage provides peace of mind for high-mileage use
items.cta: Find long-warranty rebuilt Jaguar E-Pace engines

items.question: Does servicing affect Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement warranty?
items.answer: Yes, maintaining Jaguar E-Pace engines to manufacturer service intervals using correct oil specifications is required to keep your replacement engine warranty valid. Missed services or incorrect fluids can void coverage.
keyPoints: Jaguar E-Pace warranty requires OEM-spec low-SAPS oil
Service records must be retained for warranty claims
Oil changes every 5,000-7,000 miles recommended for rebuilt units
Independent specialists can perform warranty-compliant servicing
items.cta: Book Jaguar E-Pace engine supply & fit with warranty included

items.question: When should I replace rather than repair my Jaguar E-Pace engine?
items.answer: Replace your Jaguar E-Pace engine when internal damage is confirmed-metallic knocking, low oil pressure, or timing chain jump-rather than attempting partial repairs. Rebuilt units offer better long-term value once catastrophic failure occurs.
keyPoints: Jaguar E-Pace engine knock indicates bearing damage requiring replacement
Timing chain jump usually causes valve damage beyond economical repair
Rebuilt Jaguar E-Pace engines cost less than dealer cylinder head repairs
Supply & fit packages simplify logistics versus sourcing parts separately
items.cta: Compare Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement versus repair costs

items.question: How do I verify the correct engine code for my Jaguar E-Pace?
items.answer: Enter your Jaguar E-Pace registration above to instantly identify your exact engine code, variant and compatible replacement options. VIN decoding confirms AJ200D4, 204DTD or ING-20P codes to prevent costly ordering errors.
keyPoints: Jaguar E-Pace engine codes vary by model year and emissions spec
Registration lookup prevents AJ200D4 vs 204DTD compatibility errors
X540 chassis engines not universally interchangeable across years
EngineMarket specialists verify code match before dispatch
items.cta: Verify your Jaguar E-Pace engine code instantly via registration

items.answer: Yes, vetted UK independent specialists match dealer capability for Jaguar E-Pace engine replacement, often at lower cost, using identical OEM-spec components and providing 12-month warranties without main dealer labour premiums.

Labour costs typically 30-50% lower than main dealer rates

Warranty terms identical whether dealer or independent supplies engine
